programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents examine the death of a seaman and find themselves working alongside Barrett. Gibbs makes his displeasure about the circumstances of the case well known, prompting the rest of the investigators to worry that he might be about to leave the NCIS again. Meanwhile, Ziva introduces her new boyfriend to her team-mates. Crime drama, guest starring Enrique Murciano

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy lieutenant is found dead in the Pentagon's custodial closet, and surveillance footage indicates she was killed by a co-worker. However, Ziva and the team discover her supposed assailant actually died of a heart attack days before the crime was committed. Meanwhile, suspicions are raised regarding the eyeball left in Tony's drink, and the investigators' attention is fixed on the port-to-port murderer, who appears to still be at large

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony's one-time Baltimore PD partner Danny Price is murdered in a manner that suggests the port-to-port killer has struck again. However, a key difference in the way the victim was bound suggests the crime was actually the work of a copycat, forcing the investigators to consider whether Price had any enemies

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New evidence reveals the serial killer has infiltrated the agency and is targeting a member of the NCIS team, prompting Gibbs to seek the guidance of his former mentor Mike Franks. A lead from Barrett's squad reveals the CIA has also been tracking the murderer - and its findings suggest the culprit could be an even deadlier foe than anticipated

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team attend the funeral of a fallen colleague before resuming their hunt for the man they believe to be the port-to-port killer. Vance frantically researches the suspect's background to learn more about his motivations and discover who his next target could be - but one of the investigators follows a clue that leads to danger

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Eric pulls the team together when he finds a video of kidnapped agent Dominic Vail posted on a jihadist website, with the terrorists holding him stating that he will be executed within 24 hours unless a comrade of theirs being held by the US government is released. One of the captors is identified as a being an associate of Sam, and his involvement also seems to indicate Dom is being held in Sudan. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An attack on a private plane immediately after it lands turns out to be the hijack of the secret army transfer of terrorist leader Ala A Din Keshwar, the man whose release was demanded in exchange for Dom's life. Callen and the rest of the NCIS team vow to get their hands on Keshwar before he can flee the country. Meanwhile, Hetty offers her resignation to Vance following Dom's death

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen's cover is blown while he is working undercover and he sends a coded text to Hetty, who effectively cuts him off from NCIS as she makes it the team's top priority to discover who found out his real identity and how. A man called Eugene Keelson contacts Callen and threatens to reveal information about his past unless he runs an errand for him, which involves delivering a flash drive to Bulgarian gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Keelson is found to have had a huge file labelled 'Callen, G', but it is destroyed remotely before Callen can retrieve it. The team is assigned to monitor Iranian terrorists trying to get their hands on a huge hidden fortune that belonged to the former shah, and the case appears to have a concrete link to Callen when a woman in the group's ranks is identified as being his sister Amy. Starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's knight in shining armour arrives in the form of paroled rock legend Abel Kane, whisking them off on a winter break to Majorca, where yet more unexpected good fortune awaits them. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy's wish for a quiet birthday is soon forgotten when Kezia announces she's got a party planned and he finds himself dodging bullets on the Aldbourne estate. Newly promoted DI Sam Nixon is put firmly in her place by Neil and promptly goes behind his back to revive the search for missing youngster Amy Tennant on the anniversary of her disappearance. June tells Tony and Reg she's taking early retirement

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sam's initiative on the anniversary of Amy Tennant's disappearance gives the team a lead when a friend of the family is seen stealing something from the missing child's bedroom. Emma and Diane investigate an abandoned van and unwittingly set loose a flock of frightened sheep, which turn out to be stolen stock on the way to an illegal slaughterhouse

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Emma works out that abductor Shelley Fitzgerald is likely to take Amy Tennant to her childhood haunts in Brighton. Lewis is the first to congratulate her for getting the hunt back on track, but husband Matt continues to belittle everything she does. Neil leads the team in a frantic search on the Sussex coast, where an elated James Tennant finally sets eyes on his daughter for the first time in a year

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Just two days away from retirement, June uncovers a swindle involving property developer Brian Stevens (guest star Mike Reid) and a local authority official involved in the sell-off of the Aldbourne estate. The trail leads close to home and, when DAC Georgia Hobbs is seen kissing John Heaton, June is left not knowing who she can trust

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Secret
programme.desc: Feature-length dramatisation of the writer's only thriller, set in 19th-century Tyneside. Colin Buchanan stars as successful businessman Freddie Musgrave, who has worked hard for 20 years to live down his misspent youth as runner to a gang of smugglers and is shocked when a face appears from long ago, threatening to reveal the details of his sordid past. With Clare Higgins and June Whitfield

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Round Tower
programme.desc: Feature-length drama set in the 1950s, starring Emilia Fox as the rebellious teenage daughter of a successful businessman and his devoted wife, whose disenchantment with her privileged upbringing ultimately provokes a scandal in their small town. Keith Barron and Jan Harvey also star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A diabetic teenager faces a tough time after falling pregnant, a situation complicated by her boyfriend's criminal past. Nurse Crane offers what support she can - but the girl's life is endangered when the young lovers go on the run. A case of petty theft brings a group of Irish travellers to the attention of the midwives, who end up treating a newborn infant and looking after an expectant mother, and it seems Fred's romance is on the up when he asks Violet to accompany him to a fundraising dance

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: The army prepares to make another attack on the barricades as the fighting grows more vicious and desperate. Valjean feels conflicted about Marius' relationship with Cosette and faces a heartbreaking choice when he comes to realise what is in her best interests. However, he underestimates the strength of Cosette's feelings for him. Javert is thrown into turmoil by the selfless actions of a man he has always viewed as an irredeemable criminal, and is forced to reconsider everything he believes in

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne accuses Cromwell of betrayal when she finds out he tried to protect Mary and not Elizabeth at a time of crisis, but the queen's power is dissolving rapidly and her enemies are gathering. Her sister-in-law Jane Rochford reveals to Cromwell that Mark Smeaton, Francis Weston and Henry Norris have all declared their love to Anne, which is a treasonable offence, and further insinuates that her husband George Boleyn's unhealthy sexual appetite extends to his sister. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Claire Foy and Jessica Raine

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Back on the murder squad, Pat is asked to investigate when a young mother of two, Diane Leach, is abducted from a local wood. The confession of a local sex offender complicates matters, as Pat looks into the possibility that the father of a young protege of the woman may be responsible for her disappearance. Meanwhile, Walker has worries of his own, as his ex-wife Lyn is dating an abusive, unstable man, who turns into a manic-obsessive when she decides to leave him

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah leads the charge to rescue James, whose ordeal continues when his sexuality becomes known, and puts her relationship with George at risk when a disturbing discovery forces her to reveal her darkest secret

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Boxing Day sees heavy snowfall covering the country and the adverse weather causes major disruption across Poplar as roads become blocked and pipes freeze meaning families have to queue outside for water from standpipes. Sister Julienne must inform a family about the death of their loved one and uncovers an uncomfortable truth, while Valerie helps an unmarried couple living in a caravan prepare for their baby, but unforeseen complications knock her confidence in her ability as a midwife. Guest starring Anita Dobson

Uniform search wasteland where a skeleton has been found and Lewis is surprised when the trail leads to his flatmate's brother Rudy

On a brighter note, the whole sorry business has one happy side-effect _ it helps reconcile Will with estranged wife Kate. Meanwhile, Tom and Alex's budding relationship is jeopardised by a difference of medical opinion. Starring Simon Shepherd and Maggie O'Neill

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The vets have their work cut out dealing with a sheepdog that cannot bark, and Helen takes a liking to a stray cat and kitten she comes across in the garden. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Lionel and Jean return from a weekend away to find Judith's boyfriend has been staying at the house. To add to their woes, Alistair announces he has got engaged to a girl named Mercury - dashing their hopes of his ever becoming their son-in-law. Comedy starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth plans a nautical supper aboard a borrowed cruiser, but the event does not go to plan. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Irrepressible snob Hyacinth concocts an elaborate plan to rescue Richard from social obscurity by turning him into an executive. His first stop is the local golf club, but he proves far from enthusiastic. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg climbs a tree to prove a point, but finds getting down is not as easy. Meanwhile, the police investigate the case of a runaway cooker. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: An archaeologist posing as Rosemary dies mysteriously during a dig for Anglo-Saxon artefacts in the grounds of Lord Engleton's stately home, presenting the duo with a fresh mystery to solve. The gardens are unaccountably blighted by yellowing, forcing the estate to close its gates to the public, and a second death suggests somebody will go to any lengths for a piece of British history. Guest starring Julian Wadham

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part adaptation of the second of JK Rowling's detective novels featuring private eye Cormoran Strike. The detective is hired by the wife of a notorious author to find her missing husband, only to discover the writer has been murdered in a manner remarkably similar to a scene in one of his books. Meanwhile, Robin is caught between Strike and Matthew's hopes

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The discovery of a bloodstained pair of hot pants results in the team being asked to reinvestigate the 1971 disappearance of a mother and her six-month-old son. Lane, who worked on the original case, quickly identifies the victim's husband as the chief suspect, but when Halford and Standing learn two other women went missing in the area on the same day, they suspect they are looking for a serial killer. Amanda Redman stars

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A businessman tries to undermine the British economy so Ros goes undercover at his company. When the target's assistant recognises Lucas from his days in Russia, Ros must use her powers of seduction to save the country from disaster and convince the man that he can trust her. Drama, starring Hermione Norris and Richard Armitage, with Paul Rhys

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: June is paired with ex-husband Jim on an observation mission to find out what notorious drug dealer Pat Hadley is up to in London. She discovers Jim has turned his life around and has a hidden motive for his visit from Manchester. Uniform search wasteland where a skeleton has been found and Lewis is surprised when the trail leads to his flatmate's brother Rudy

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Newly demoted Havers joins Lynley on a double murder investigation. After a girl is killed and her camping companion stabbed, two separate lines of inquiry lead the duo into a murky world of deception, corruption and prostitution in their bid to discover the murderer's identity. Mystery drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small, Timothy West and Simon Williams

programme.desc: June's retirement party is put on hold when she and her ex-husband are taken hostage by the drugs gang. programme.desc: Pauline sees her children in a new light, Arthur receives some threatening mail but vows to fight back. Nigel can't tell Clare the truth and Ian's compliments to Cindy are unwelcome

programme.title: Peak Practice

Medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Gray O'Brien and Maggie O'Neill

programme.desc: Jean and Lionel settle down to enjoy a quiet evening, only to find themselves constantly disturbed by ominous noises from the empty house next door. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.desc: Richard has an accident and Hyacinth decides to buy a small country retreat - but her plans go awry in a most unexpected fashion. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth learns the Barker-Finches had a local celebrity at their barbecue, and is desperate to outdo them with her own event - an 'outdoors indoors luxury barbecue and finger buffet'. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.desc: Roy Barraclough guest stars as Crowcroft, a well-known Lothario with a preference for gullible women. When he samples Nora Batty's baking, he decides to make her his next target. Long-running comedy, with Kathy Staff, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: The Da Vinci Code (2006)
programme.desc: A respected academic goes on the run when he is framed for the murder of a French historian. Pursued by the detective on the case and a sinister assassin monk, he strives to unmask the culprit, leading to a conspiracy determined to preserve a 2,000-year-old secret at any cost. Thriller based on Dan Brown's novel, starring Tom Hanks, Audrey Tautou, Ian McKellen, Paul Bettany and Jean Reno

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Halford visits a clairvoyant in the hope of making contact with his dead wife, but instead receives a message from a teenage girl who was abducted and left to die in a transport container in 1982. Despite the unconventional nature of the new evidence, Pullman agrees to reopen the investigation. The ageing detectives become convinced it will be their final case when they are ordered to report for physical and psychological evaluation. James Bolam, Amanda Redman and Robert Bathurst star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A teenager holds the key to an assassination plot having witnessed a murder. After being sent to protect him, Lucas discovers the threat comes from inside the British security services. programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live on coral reefs, including groupers, small predators that scientists believe are as intelligent as chimpanzees. These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team launches Plant Britain, an ambitious two-year challenge aimed at encouraging everyone in the nation to get planting - no matter where they live or how much space they have. Matt Baker is in Cheshire planting the very first trees in what will become the programmes Plant Britain wood, while Anita Rani is in her hometown of Bradford to help residents of the Canterbury estate to plant trees in their front gardens and at a local primary school

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide which pair will leave the contest. Plus, a performance by Gary Barlow

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: In search of the knife, Will and Lyra try to gain entry into the Torre Degli Angeli, and discover a tied-up Giacomo. In Oxford, Boreal's offer of private funding is refused by Mary, while Lee finds Grumman - or Jopari as he is now known - and they take to the skies in search of the bearer. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Black forensic scientist Leroy Logan decides to join the Metropolitan Police in the hope of changing racist attitudes in the force from within its ranks. Leroy has to not only face the consequences of his father's disapproval, but also the blatant racism he finds in his new role as a despised yet exemplary constable in the Met. Fact-based drama by Courttia Newland and Steve McQueen, starring John Boyega and Steve Toussaint

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, including Southampton v Manchester United at St Mary's Stadium and Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur at Stamford Bridge. The Saints held United to a 1-1 draw in last season's corresponding fixture and will have fancied their changes of gaining at least a point once again given their strong start to the season, but most of United's best results this term have come on their travels. The London rivals will both have aspirations of challenging for the title and the Blues will also be hoping to avenge a penalty shootout defeat at the hands of Spurs at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in the Carabao Cup

programme.title: Defending Digga D
programme.desc: Documentary following the 20-year-old drill artist after his release from a 15-month stint in prison for violent disorder, intent on making a success of his music career

programme.title: Coriolanus (2011)
programme.desc: A feared general is manipulated by his scheming mother into pursuing a political career, but his failure to win public approval results in riots and his banishment. Furious at the society that has cast him aside, he allies with an old enemy to get revenge. Shakespearean drama, directed by and starring Ralph Fiennes. With Gerard Butler and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast. Also in HD

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts explore a commercial building in Beeston, Nottinghamshire, a four-storey house in East Dulwich and a flat in Godalming in Surrey

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A fraudster is left bankrupt and behind bars when his attempt at a crash-for-cash scam backfires. Questions are raised after a stolen watch is found, but not where it should be

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: There is an expensive decision to be made for a dog owner at out-of-hours vet practice, while a team tries to repair a section of railway track before the morning commute

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles finds out about the history of the humble spoon

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma has a date with Aashiq, while Bear and Sid receive an indecent proposal. Daniel is caught up in wedding day drama when an all-clear from cancer prompts the bride to question whether she even wants to get married

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a retired lecturer search for a property in the countryside of Northumberland on a budget of £200,000. Away from the hunt, the presenter visits a castle

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, then she and two experts transform a sideboard, a cane chair and cider demi-johns

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team try to restore an ornate walnut piano stool, a speedometer from a 1920s vintage car and a baby's wicker crib

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents coverage of the third-round draw, as the remaining Football League and non-league teams discover if they have been given a money-spinning tie against a Premier League or Championship club. Last year, Liverpool were paired with Merseyside rivals Everton, while AFC Fylde were drawn away to Sheffield United

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick and Katy visit the care home where he grew up, and while she is nervous about the memories it could trigger in him, he is determined to get answers. Back in the Square, Linda sets up an anniversary party for her husband - but his reaction leaves her devastated, leaving Max to comfort her. Bobby asks Ian to choose a winner for the Lucy Beale award - but his dad is too busy as usual

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Songs of Praise crew visits Dibley to record a programme in the village church. However, when Geraldine develops a crush on the producer her wayward emotions threaten to plunge the proceedings into disaster. Comedy, guest starring Peter Capaldi (The Thick of It)

programme.title: Anton Ferdinand: Football, Racism & Me
programme.desc: The former professional footballer goes on a deeply personal journey to explore the issue of racial abuse in the game, talking about his own highly-publicised 2011 incident with ex-England captain John Terry. Anton confronts how the ripple effects of the incident and the online abuse he experienced not only affected his own mental health and career but also the lives of his loved ones. He reveals where he believes players are still being failed by the system, and the ways in which football urgently needs to change

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Sammyjo has to deal with a busy salon and staff illness, while Kendal juggles being a young mum with work and has a surprise for Sammyjo. The demand for post-lockdown haircuts continues

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The pressure of running a busy salon during a pandemic gets to Sammyjo, but there is good news for two members of the team

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Reginald D Hunter and Charlene White joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to music superstar Mariah Carey about her eponymous Magical Christmas Special. TV chef and writer Nadiya Hussain reflects on new book Nadiya Bakes, while British astronaut Tim Peake talks about his autobiography Limitless. Pointless co-presenter Richard Osman discusses his debut crime novel The Thursday Murder Club. Singer-songwriter Gary Barlow also enjoys a brief natter before he performs the single Incredible, from his new solo album Music Played by Humans

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a Victorian house in London's Crystal Palace, a chalet bungalow in Rackheath near Norwich, and a terrace house in Canterbury

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An opportunist tries his hand at forgery to sign off his own claim, and a case of road rage escalates into a fraudulent claim

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: The team at a new power station burns rubbish through the night to generate electricity, and road workers have to get blacktop laid before the morning rush in torrential rain

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from the Westpoint Arena in Exeter, where Nick Hall and Richard Madley are the experts, and Eric also finds out more about Elizabethan slander and cursing

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen and Rob reassure Tom and Ella after a crisis, but the kids are keeping something from them. A homeless man convinces Jimmi that a senior police officer is corrupt

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a creative couple looking to start a new life in rural Devon on a budget of £375,000 and visits a vast collection of military memorabilia

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph restores a trolley at Earlswood Recycling Centre, while Chloe Kempster overhauls a vintage table and chest and Duncan McKean transforms an antique floor lamp

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a fairground water can, a camel saddle and a RAF mascot teddy bear, restored as a tribute to the airman it belonged to

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian gleefully warns Max he now has the upper hand on him - unless Max wants the whole Square to know who he has been kissing. Tina visits Mick, who is despondent after his day at the care home, but she assumes he is upset because of losing the Vic and sets out to get rid of Ian for good. Meanwhile, Jack and Whitney warn Tiffany about her surrogacy plans and Thompson confronts Callum

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha makes a controversial decision while operating on a cancerous patient, which leaves Max, Donna and Dom all concerned about his actions and general welfare - but their interference results in him taking out his frustrations on Jodie. Cameron tries to get back on a furious Chloe's good side, but his actions have disastrous consequences for a patient. Clayton demands that Kian come clean about his drug habit

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Richard Bilton reports on the businesses fighting to survive the pandemic as customers are left unable to make use of their services, from the adventure playground with no children to an events company with no events. Filmed over the course of the first and second lockdowns, this programme reveals the pressure on those trying to save jobs and livelihoods.

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The four chefs competing in this round are challenged to prepare either potato rosti served with egg, bacon and tomatoes or tempura mussels with a smoked garlic aioli for their skills test. For the second round, they have one hour and 25 minutes to cook their own two-course signature dishes and secure themselves a place in the quarter finals

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Fraser and Caitlin become close, but even as they grow to understand each other, they remain unsure about their own sexual identities. Their friendship creates a safe space in which Caitlin starts to consider the idea of a sex change, while Fraser develops a crush on a soldier in his mother's chain of command

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Soldier Craig is about to be deployed on a mission in Afghanistan. The feeling of impending danger and the imminent departure of a key member of their group push the kids to embrace the present with unrestrained urgency, as they organise an impromptu send-off party at an abandoned villa

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter U, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Joe Crowley are in the Lincolnshire wolds at historic South Ormsby, which has ambitions to be the finest farming estate in the world. They discover the entrepreneurial spirit driving the revival of the local rural economy, including two young women carving out new careers. Elsewhere, Adam Henson has high hopes for an inner-city teenage jockey competing in her first race, while Tom Heap investigates whether it's time to ditch the green belt

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden visits Essex, where she experiments with triangular planting and has tea and cake at Beth Chatto Gardens. Plus, at RHS Hyde Hall she moves rocks with a former student, and then sails across the Thames to the Blackwater Estuary

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: During March and April, the horticulturist clears the detritus left behind by winter, as celandines, violets and primroses begin to bloom. She plants the first seeds of the year and is joined by a local gardener to prune the box hedge, but their work is interrupted by a nesting sparrow

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Euan McIlwraith heads to the west coast, investigating the risks of locating open water salmon farms in more exposed sites. Dougie Vipond is in Angus at the Montrose Basin, an important nature reserve, to help monitor its wintering birds. Anne Lundon follows the story of the green and white Scotways signs that point out rights of access routes across the Scottish countryside, while journalist Lesley Riddoch discusses her passion for hutting

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Like many people, Beechgrove's Kirsty Wilson is unable to afford a house with a garden. She shows how to grow stylishly and be green in a small home, from hangers to houseplants

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the latest last-64 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Top seed Ronnie O'Sullivan was in dominant form at this stage last year, overcoming Tian Pengfei 6-0. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Lewis are at Herstmonceux Castle in East Sussex, where a Chinese wall plaque catches James's eye, and Paul Martin explores the history of the building

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents highlights of the second-round ties in the world's oldest domestic cup football competition, including Marine v Havant & Waterlooville, Shrewsbury Town v Oxford City and Bristol Rovers v Darlington

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a bakery that produces 180,000 Cornish pasties a day, and discovers the very specific rule one of the pastries must comply with to genuinely be considered Cornish. Ruth Goodman debunks some myths about pasties and investigates the history of the pepper trade, while Cherry Healey learns about the science of onions - including why cutting them can bring tears to the eyes

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of more business ideas. Up for consideration are a mother and son's range of spicy street food, a range of cocktails, and a former teacher's plan for a new system to mark students' schoolwork. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: The Trials of Oscar Pistorius
programme.desc: Six weeks into the trial, the defendant takes the stand, but his unconvincing testimony in the face of tough questioning leads to the charge being increased from culpable homicide to murder following an appeal. When a guilty verdict is returned, further appeals lead to his sentence rising from five year to 15

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Award-winning stand-up comedian Dane Baptiste plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by New York comic Desiree Burch and Liverpool funnyman Chris McCausland

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya presents action from the day's last-64 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of one of the day's best last-64 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Max wrestles with the after-effects of neurosurgery and the financial pressure on the hospital, but things get worse when an encounter with an old enemy puts a strain on her relationship with Louis. Kian brings in some new recruits to help solve Jac's medical mystery, and a new arrival stirs up unpleasant memories for Donna

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites actor and comedian Ben Miller, broadcaster Grace Dent, comedian Desiree Burch and presenter Graham Norton to share their favourite books, reveal the books they pretend to have read but haven't and the book they would give someone on a first date. The panellists also discuss this week's book group pick, The Devil and the Dark Water by Stuart Turton, as well as Graham Norton's latest novel, Home Stretch

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks are in Leominster, Herefordshire, to help two teams look for items, while presenter Christina Trevanion learns about local photographer Derek Evans

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling expert Jay Blades uses his salvaging expertise at Altrincham Recycling Centre to search for three items that he can clean up and reinvent. Blacksmith Kev Paxton takes on the challenge of a metal window box in need of a complete makeover, while design team Josh Kennard and Oli Allen are challenged with finding a new use for some stepladders. Jay sees the value in some old tiles - but will he be able to turn all three items into money makers? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: As winter morphs into spring, lambing and calving keeps the team busy out on the farms. However, their small animal clinic is also packed with pets and Martin Fitzpatrick - the man who founded the practice 35 years ago - faces a difficult case concerning a Highland terrier. Young vet Darren is called out on an emergency to a farm where a prize cow is struggling to deliver a calf. An examination reveals it's simply too big to come out naturally. Darren decides the safest option, for both the cow and the calf, is to perform an emergency Caesarean section. It's a risky operation, but without it, the cow will die

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the third episode, Mary visits Ireland, where wholesome comfort food is at the heart of their cuisine. Here, she makes a recipes inspired by the Emerald Isle, including a salmon and fennel one-pot wonder, and slow roast pork with crackling and buttery Colcannon mash

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the opening last-32 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Eventual champion Ding Junhui overcame a stern test of his credentials at this stage last year when he defeated Ali Carter 6-4. that victory set up a last-16 meeting with Ronnie O'Sullivan, who prevailed 6-2 against Noppon Saengkham. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn presents live coverage of Nicola Sturgeon's speech from the SNP conference in Edinburgh

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the opening last-32 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Eventual champion Ding Junhui overcame a stern test of his credentials at this stage last year when he defeated Ali Carter 6-4. that victory set up a last-16 meeting with Ronnie O'Sullivan, who prevailed 6-2 against Noppon Saengkham. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Bristol Cathedral. Antique experts James Lewis and Philip Serrell find objects to take to auction, including a bronze statue of Buddha

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: With the latest couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off at the weekend, and there are interviews with the remaining pairs and backstage gossip

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on the specialist subjects of Joe Calzaghe, the Beach Boys, Joseph Stalin, and the Gospel of John, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as first-round losers the Whitcombes and the Severns get another chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues comprising Picacho Peak, La Brea Tar Pits, Sierra Nevada Mountains, and Rio Grande River

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes featuring one of her favourite ingredients - the anchovy. She prepares a creamy dressing for the fish, served with bitter leaves and walnuts, and then creates an elixir, combining silvery salted anchovies with garlic and oil. This not only makes an excellent dip for red chicory, but also the perfect accompaniment to a platter of sliced seared steak. Nigella creates an anchovy pasta dish of spaghetti with chard and chilli, and also makes a lemon blossom cocktail and a lemon and elderflower pudding

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: A bed shortage in Barnet hospital highlights the perpetual challenge of trying to discharge older patients, alongside the added complexities of Covid-19 infection risks. As autumn begins, the hospital's emergency department is seeing a resurgence of patients after a summer of low attendances, with more people arriving in A&E than almost any other day since last winter, and they are admitting more patients than they are discharging

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Grace Dent and Nathan Caton to take a light-hearted look at the year's cultural highlights, including Take That's comeback, Daniel Craig's debut as James Bond in Casino Royale and the release of Amy Winehouse's album Back to Black

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from the day's last-32 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents extended highlights of one of the day's best last-32 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Joe Crowley are in the Lincolnshire wolds at historic South Ormsby, which has ambitions to be the finest farming estate in the world. They discover the entrepreneurial spirit driving the revival of the local rural economy, including two young women carving out new careers. Elsewhere, Adam Henson has high hopes for an inner-city teenage jockey competing in her first race, while Tom Heap investigates whether it's time to ditch the green belt

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Scunthorpe woman Caroline Blanchard, a single mum who has twice had to make the decision to turn off the life support for people she loves. She and her children live in the shadow of the garage, where seven-year-old Natasha banged her head in 2007, and the conservatory, where husband and father Paul had a heart attack four years later. The volunteers plan is to give them a new home for a new start, including demolishing the conservatory and garage where these two tragedies occurred

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Marcus Waring challenges two of today's four chefs to butcher a chicken, debone the thighs and cook them with asparagus and a brandy, mushroom and mustard cream sauce, all in just 20 minutes. The others are set a task by Monica Galetti, and must create a fruit tart filled with a berry compote, piped pistachio chantilly and candied pistachios

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Anti-fraud teams deal with a crash-for-cash duo when an £8 million scam is exposed, and food poisoning leaves one insurer with a claim for eight holiday-makers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, then she and two experts transform a sideboard, a cane chair and cider demi-johns

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: Born in 1962 to Nigerian parents studying medicine in London, Kemi Nevins now runs a successful cafe in Abercynon serving up delicious food. However, none of that she offers reflects her Nigerian background. Now Kemi's children are adults themselves, they would like to know more about their heritage, so the Welsh-Italian cook visits Kemi to help her reconnect to her roots through her love of food

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan meets people working in the gas industry in and around the Cumbrian coast. She travels by helicopter to the Morecambe gas field as engineers find new ways of extracting the remaining fuel supply that has been exploited for more than 35 years. Keeley also heads for the Barrow gas terminal where a complex network of pipes sends the gas to local homes

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the latest last-32 matches from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Then world champion Judd Trump suffered a shock defeat at this stage last year when he was beaten 6-3 by veteran Nigel Bond. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt are in Glemham Hall, Suffolk, where they uncover a Victorian peddler doll complete with wicker basket, while Paul Martin explores Framlingham Castle

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents all the backstage gossip from Strictly Come Dancing, as well as the latest traumas

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Olympic gold medal-winning swimmer Rebecca Adlington and TV presenter Helen Skelton rummage for items of interest - and value - in the north west of England, assisted by antique experts Roo Irvine and Tim Medhurst. En route to auction in Bolton, they discover the salty secret of Cheshire cheese and hear the story of how, thanks to a Hawaiian Duke, swimming was changed for ever

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: Andi Oliver oversees proceedings as previous winners of the culinary contest prepare a feast for the heroes of the pandemic - beginning with a course of canapes. In a twist on previous series, the eight cooks will be split into two teams of four and will judge the other group's efforts in a blind taste test. Michelin-starred chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen will be joining the teams in the kitchen

programme.title: QI XS
programme.desc: Highlights compiled from recent series of QI, the comedy quiz hosted by Sandi Toksvig

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Robert throws a narcotic-fuelled gathering for Harper's birthday and the pair stay up all night in a haze of drink and drugs. Harper heads into work the next day and is horrified to learn she has made a trading error that will cost the desk £50,000, and desperately attempts to fix her mistake in fear of losing her job

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe introduces a special festive edition of the comedy show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by Chris McCausland and musical comedy double act Flo & Joan

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from the day's last-32 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship Extra
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents extended highlights of one of the day's best last-32 matches at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: With sky high property prices, home ownership seems out of reach to many. The government's shared ownership scheme is supposed to help get people on the property ladder by letting them buy part of their home and rent the rest, but this programme hears from shared owners who say uncapped service charges, building defects and costly lease extensions have left them stuck in homes with escalating costs and huge debts

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The presenter explores some of the myths swirling around the French Revolution of 1789 - an uprising that brought down the nation's royal family. This violent revolution became the blueprint of many future revolutions across the world, but what exactly happened during this turbulent period is open to manipulation and interpretation. Historian Michael Rapport explains how the revolution was not started by starving peasants as many assume but was in fact sparked by a group of lawyers and property owners

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. The first two chefs must French trim a rack of Lamb and cook a portion served with a mint pesto, with the second pair of competitors asked to fillet a plaice and cook one portion, and serve with a chicken butter sauce and pickled radish. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Crossing England in a Punt: River of Dreams
programme.desc: Tom Fort embarks on a 170-mile journey down the River Trent in his own custom-built punt. The presenter encounters the power stations that generate much of the nation's electricity, meets veterans of the floods of 1947 and learns about a Bronze Age boatman who made a life on the banks as he discovers the waterway's 3,000-year history

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: From enormous tortoises and deep-diving lizards to fish-eating snakes and birds that hunt giant, venomous centipedes, the wildlife of the Galapagos Islands is unique and bizarre. However, the wilderness that inspired Charles Darwin and the theory of evolution is becoming ever-more crowded due to increased migration and tourism. David Attenborough narrates this documentary, which examines how the Pacific archipelago's animals are adapting to their changing circumstances

programme.title: Scotland: Rome's Final Frontier
programme.desc: Archaeologist Dr Fraser Hunter reflects on three centuries of interaction and conflict between the Romans and ancient Scottish tribes. He examines whether the Scots were too strong for the invading army or if the country was simply considered not worth the effort of conquering, and reveals a story of a superpower pitted against tribesmen and warlords, and one with fascinating modern parallels

programme.title: Balloon (2018)
programme.desc: Thuringen, East Germany in 1979, and like many of the country's citizens, the Strelzyk family are desperate to escape to the west. Under the watchful eye of the Stasi and the fearsome lieutenant colonel Seidel, they try to cross the border in a homemade hot air balloon. Drama, starring Friedrich Mucke and Alicia von Rittberg. In German with English subtitles

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on April 12, with performances by artists including Jesus Jones, Technotronic featuring MC, Janet Jackson, Heart, Bizz Nizz, Paula Abdul, the Cure, Madonna and the Quireboys

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on April 19, with performances by artists including Sonia, the Quireboys, Adamski, the Blues Brothers, Alannah Myles, the Adventures of Stevie V, Faith No More, the Family Stand and Madonna

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: In the run-up to next month's reopening of the writer's former country home in the Scottish Borders, Stuart Kelly goes behind-the-scenes as more than 13,000 treasures are moved back in to Abbotsford House. He also examines the controversy surrounding the bizarre building that Scott himself designed

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Two men claiming to be IRA sympathisers approach Thomas in the Garrison and offer to buy the missing crate of guns they have heard he has in his possession. The gang leader also goes to Cheltenham races with Grace, where he aims to persuade kingpin Billy Kimber to make the Peaky Blinders part of his operation, while Ada secretly marries communist agitator Freddie Thorne. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The war with the Lees escalates when they ransack the Shelbys' betting shop, while John reveals he intends to remarry, but his choice of bride does not go down well with his family. Chief Inspector Campbell puts more pressure on Thomas to deliver Freddie Thorne and the crate of stolen guns to him, while Grace begins to glean more information about the Peaky Blinders' illegal operations

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: The steeplejack takes a look at the extraordinary expansion of industrial Britain in the 18th and 19th centuries, and the continued use of huge steam engines in collieries, steelworks and mills in the 20th century

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a beautiful monochromatic scene with delicate leafless trees and a rich grassy meadow cuddling a sweet cottage

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Bendor Grosvenor and Emma Dabiri continue their search for lost and hidden public treasures. The pair travel to Carmarthenshire in Wales to investigate two intriguing portraits of a local nobleman and his wife, the Earl and Countess of Carber, which they suspect were painted by Sir Peter Lely in the 17th century

programme.title: Mary Beard's Shock of the Nude
programme.desc: Part one of two. The classicist examines the nude in western art and its troubling power to provoke ideas about gender, sex and moral transgression. Mary starts with a statue of Aphrodite by Praxiteles and Titian's Venus of Urbino, asking how a woman like her should respond to the artworks, and considers the challenge of depicting the female nude for a woman artist by looking at the work of 17th-century painter Artemisia Gentileschi. In a life-drawing class, Mary joins a hen party as they attempt to sketch a naked man

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Film-maker Xavier Alford talks to other people who have Guillain-Barre syndrome as he attempts to come to terms with being diagnosed as having the condition, which the director had been hiding from family and close friends. He is confronted with the seriousness of the illness when some of the patients he meets are on the edge of life, locked in their own bodies, completely paralysed and unable to move a single muscle, while their brains remain unaffected

programme.title: Robert Burns - The People's Poet
programme.desc: Andrew O'Hagan celebrates the life of Scotland's most famous poet, and explores the reasons why Burns remains such a cultural force. The author examines the politics, patriotism and sense of place of the man known in his native land as the Bard

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas deals with an IRA chief who has come to Small Heath to avenge his cousin's death. Meanwhile, Campbell gets closer to finding the stolen guns and Grace has to decide whether her loyalties lie with the inspector or with Thomas. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas prepares to oust Billy Kimber at Worcester Racecourse, but secrets are revealed that force the Shelbys to face up to the problems that have divided them. Meanwhile, Campbell unleashes one last plan to destroy the Peaky Blinders and Grace finds herself with an impossible decision to make. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill

programme.title: Mary Beard's Shock of the Nude
programme.desc: Part one of two. The classicist examines the nude in western art and its troubling power to provoke ideas about gender, sex and moral transgression. Mary starts with a statue of Aphrodite by Praxiteles and Titian's Venus of Urbino, asking how a woman like her should respond to the artworks, and considers the challenge of depicting the female nude for a woman artist by looking at the work of 17th-century painter Artemisia Gentileschi. In a life-drawing class, Mary joins a hen party as they attempt to sketch a naked man

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Age of Steam
programme.desc: Fred looks at early experiments in the use of steam for road transport, more than 100 years before the invention of the petrol engine. Plus, an insight into the development of the traction engine for agriculture and haulage

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another picture, creating a mountain scene with soft, sloping grass cover, a crystal lake and rocky banks

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: The historian continues his personal review of the 1980s, considering how by the middle of the decade, Britain was wrestling with two contradictory impulses - the rise of a strong nationalist sentiment and the emergence of an increasingly globalised world. Along the way Sandbrook examines everything from the invasion of the Falkland Islands to the invasion of the home computer. He examines the moral panic surrounding video nasties, looks at the Americanisation of UK culture and the picket-line skirmishes playing nightly on TV, as well as the Aids epidemic and the threat of the IRA

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: A look at how Margaret Thatcher's struggling public image was transformed in 1982 when Argentine forces occupied the British overseas territory of the Falkland Islands in the South Atlantic, with the triumph of the British forces making her reputation in the UK and around the world. On the home front she faced another challenge to her leadership from National Union of Miners, which called a UK-wide strike, while in 1984 the IRA bombed the Grand Hotel in Brighten in an attempt to kill her and her most senior colleagues

programme.title: The Falklands Play
programme.desc: Production of Ian Curteis's controversial drama which charts the political manoeuvring that went on behind the scenes of Margaret Thatcher's government during the Falklands War. Patricia Hodge stars as the prime minister, with James Fox and John Standing. First aired in 2002

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found murdered at the abandoned boys' home, and her body has similar wounds to those found in the previous two killings. Kata and Arnar are under pressure to identify and capture the killer. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Kata, Arnar and the Reykjavik team examine boxes of documents from an inquiry into Icelandic children's homes in an effort to identify both the next possible victim and the killer. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

Mimi also introduces sisters Angel, Melody and Nevaeh, who are super-proud of their traveller heritage and go trick or treating on their plot of land. programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: What is it like when your mum becomes your dad? Twelve-year-old Tilly’s mum came out as transgender when she was 7 years old. We follow Tilly as she shows us what her life is like now. Having just moved up to secondary school, Tilly hasn't told her new friends about her family as she is uncertain as to when will be the right time to tell them. When her birthday party comes up, Tilly has a decision to make - will she explain to her friends that Jack is trans or will she make up a story to buy herself more time? To help her decide, Tilly meets Nat and Faith, who both have trans dads and help her feel more confident about sharing her story. And when younger brother Mal experiences some mean comments at school, new friend Nat offers to give a talk to his class.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Lars and Kyan swim through the shallows of Crystal River in Florida. Kyan tests his new swimming fin, while Lars watches a manatee and her baby cruise by. Xuli is water-skiing further downstream in the open waters of Crystal Bay, while Foz and Ubercorn watch on from Jet Pad. Grandmaster Glitch is taking the tourist family on a mermaid spotting tour of Crystal River aboard his Grimmer-boat. As Glitch heads off in search of real mermaids, he accidentally startles the mother manatee. Ubercorn realises the manatee will be in danger from the speeding boats and from the cold deep water. The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. The baby manatee has become attached to Kyan, so Lars suggests he remains with her, while Lars and Foz go to save the mother. Kyan is not happy at having to play baby-sitter.

programme.desc: While Xuli and Kyan brush up on their Vroomster skills, Tala, Lars and Foz help the Academy’s banana crop by removing some vines that were constricting the trees. Nearby, Glitch prepares a picnic for Ubercorn. Tala drops by to deliver some fresh bananas for the picnic and Foz spots a tiny ant on the fruit. Tala asks her Uncle to shrink her, Foz and Lars with his size-ray. The tiny Go Jetters discover the ants will accept them as one of their own if they rub the insects’ smell on them. Now they are no longer a threat, the team follows the ants into their tree. Ubercorn and Glitch tuck into their picnic, but an ant steals the cherry from Glitch’s cake and takes it inside the tree. Unable fit inside, Glitch uses his size-ray to shrink himself and zooms after the ant. Inside the tree, the ants see Glitch as a threat and start to surround him. He makes a run for it, but they pursue him down the trunk. Glitch demands the Grimbots make him bigger again, but they miss and accidentally blast an

programme.title: Wales and the History of the World
programme.desc: Eddie Butler continues his exploration of the stories behind objects in Welsh museums, focusing on the country's contribution on international trade and industry, from the woollen sleeping bags for the Russian army, to the tinplate that kept food fresh for the pioneers of the Wild West

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team launches Plant Britain, an ambitious two-year challenge aimed at encouraging everyone in the nation to get planting - no matter where they live or how much space they have. Matt Baker is in Cheshire planting the very first trees in what will become the programmes Plant Britain wood, while Anita Rani is in her hometown of Bradford to help residents of the Canterbury estate to plant trees in their front gardens and at a local primary school

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: In search of the knife, Will and Lyra try to gain entry into the Torre Degli Angeli, and discover a tied-up Giacomo. In Oxford, Boreal's offer of private funding is refused by Mary, while Lee finds Grumman - or Jopari as he is now known - and they take to the skies in search of the bearer. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Black forensic scientist Leroy Logan decides to join the Metropolitan Police in the hope of changing racist attitudes in the force from within its ranks. Leroy has to not only face the consequences of his father's disapproval, but also the blatant racism he finds in his new role as a despised yet exemplary constable in the Met. Fact-based drama by Courttia Newland and Steve McQueen, starring John Boyega and Steve Toussaint

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: George Shotton wed Mamie Stuart in 1918, despite already being a married man. Though suspected of Mamie's disappearance a year later, he evaded conviction. It wasn't until 1961, when divers discovered human remains in a cave near Caswell Bay, that he was posthumously sentenced. The team looks at a 1934 incident known as the Brighton Trunk Murder, in which a dismembered body was found split between two trunks. Given the similarities between this murder and that of Mamie Stuart, the team believes that Shotton could be a prime suspect for this murder too

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: Award-winning comedy, written by and starring Caroline Aherne and Craig Cash, following the lives of a working-class Manchester family and set almost exclusively in their living room. With Ricky Tomlinson and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Another leading performer on the stand-up circuit plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by fellow comedians ready to share their light-hearted views on modern life

programme.title: Coriolanus (2011)
programme.desc: A feared general is manipulated by his scheming mother into pursuing a political career, but his failure to win public approval results in riots and his banishment. Furious at the society that has cast him aside, he allies with an old enemy to get revenge. Shakespearean drama, directed by and starring Ralph Fiennes. With Gerard Butler and Vanessa Redgrave

Bobby asks Ian to choose a winner for the Lucy Beale award - but his dad is too busy as usual

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: As the pandemic continues to destroy lives and livelihoods, Wyre Davies meets some of those whose futures are on the line in this BBC Wales Investigates programme. programme.title: Anton Ferdinand: Football, Racism & Me
programme.desc: The former professional footballer goes on a deeply personal journey to explore the issue of racial abuse in the game, talking about his own highly-publicised 2011 incident with ex-England captain John Terry. Anton confronts how the ripple effects of the incident and the online abuse he experienced not only affected his own mental health and career but also the lives of his loved ones. He reveals where he believes players are still being failed by the system, and the ways in which football urgently needs to change

programme.title: Sweet Sixteen: A Transgender Story
programme.desc: An intimate coming-of-age film following the life of transgender teenager Llyr Jones as she turns 16 and moves from childhood into adulthood. The documentary captures some of the key moments in her progression, offering a unique insight into the teen's experiences on the path to transitioning

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Sammyjo has to deal with a busy salon and staff illness, while Kendal juggles being a young mum with work and has a surprise for Sammyjo. The demand for post-lockdown haircuts continues

programme.desc: Ian gleefully warns Max he now has the upper hand on him - unless Max wants the whole Square to know who he has been kissing. A young boy with leukaemia has a scare when his temperature plummets, but his sparky personality has the nurses smiling and joining in a dancing contest

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The four chefs competing in this round are challenged to prepare either potato rosti served with egg, bacon and tomatoes or tempura mussels with a smoked garlic aioli for their skills test. programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Richard Bilton reports on the businesses fighting to survive the pandemic as customers are left unable to make use of their services, from the adventure playground with no children to an events company with no events. Filmed over the course of the first and second lockdowns, this programme reveals the pressure on those trying to save jobs and livelihoods.

programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Fraser and Caitlin become close, but even as they grow to understand each other, they remain unsure about their own sexual identities. programme.title: We Are Who We Are
programme.desc: Soldier Craig is about to be deployed on a mission in Afghanistan. programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live on coral reefs, including groupers, small predators that scientists believe are as intelligent as chimpanzees. These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space

programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures, including Southampton v Manchester United at St Mary's Stadium and Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur at Stamford Bridge. The London rivals will both have aspirations of challenging for the title and the Blues will also be hoping to avenge a penalty shootout defeat at the hands of Spurs at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in the Carabao Cup

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: On the islands of Orkney and Shetland, the judges visit a home inspired by Vikings, a converted ruined steading, and a maritime-themed fisherman's cottage

programme.title: Stormborn
programme.desc: Ewan McGregor narrates a documentary following animals surviving and thriving in countries on the northern edges of the Atlantic Ocean, beginning as winter releases its grip. A single Arctic fox scurries across the mountains of Iceland on a mission to re-unite with its mate from last year, while on Shetland an otter has successfully raised twin cubs to the brink of independence

programme.title: Anton Ferdinand: Football, Racism & Me
programme.desc: The former professional footballer goes on a deeply personal journey to explore the issue of racial abuse in the game, talking about his own highly-publicised 2011 incident with ex-England captain John Terry. Anton confronts how the ripple effects of the incident and the online abuse he experienced not only affected his own mental health and career but also the lives of his loved ones. He reveals where he believes players are still being failed by the system, and the ways in which football urgently needs to change

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Sammyjo has to deal with a busy salon and staff illness, while Kendal juggles being a young mum with work and has a surprise for Sammyjo. The demand for post-lockdown haircuts continues
